steps.title: Check the mean
steps.content: The removed value, $78.2^\circ\text{F}$, is the high value from the histogram. Removing a value above the original mean must make the mean of the remaining data set lower, so statement I must be true.

steps.title: Check the median
steps.content: The original data set has $11$ values, so its median is the $6$th sorted value. After removing the largest value, the new data set has $10$ values, so its median is the average of the old $5$th and $6$th sorted values. Those two values could be equal, so the median does not have to be less.

steps.title: Select the true statement
steps.content: Only statement I must be true. The answer is A.
